ogTitle: St Arnaud North vs St Clair: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Arnaud North and St Clair.
heading: St Arnaud North vs St Clair — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Arnaud North, Victoria and St Clair, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Clair has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to St Arnaud North (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Arnaud North or St Clair safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Arnaud North has a flood risk score of 58/100, while St Clair scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Arnaud North scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. St Clair scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Arnaud North or St Clair?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Clair has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
